narrative.summary: Michelin 1-star omakase. Chef Masayoshi Baba's 24-seat jewel-box — Edomae-style nigiri using BC bounty. Counter seats are the move. Vancouver's definitive omakase experience. Worth a deliberate stop for John’s design/architecture/art/food radar.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in Fraserhood, a neighborhood that still feels more lived-in than designed, which makes stepping into Masayoshi's 24-seat room feel like discovering something you weren't supposed to know about. The hinoki cypress counter curves around the open kitchen—get one of those seats—and the whole space has that pared-back Japanese restraint where every surface, every sight line, has been thought through. The lighting is low enough to feel intimate but bright enough to see the fish, which is the entire point.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Chef Masayoshi Baba works in the Edomae tradition—aged fish, warm rice, minimal interference—but sources almost everything from BC waters and farms, plus select cuts flown in from Japan and Nova Scotia. You're here for nigiri that lands somewhere between textbook precision and regional improvisation: fat Hokkaido scallops with an almost sweet meatiness, whatever's best at the dock that morning, rice seasoned and served at body temperature. It's omakase only, which means you're committing to the full progression and the price that comes with Michelin recognition—expect to spend accordingly for 15-20 pieces and a few small courses.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is the place for a milestone dinner where the food matters more than the scene, or when you want to sit at a counter and watch someone who's spent decades perfecting a very specific thing. Book the counter seats if you can—half the experience is watching Baba's hands.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We celebrated my husband’s birthday at Masayoshi, and it was an unforgettable experience from start to finish. This was our family’s very first omakase, and Chef Masayoshi exceeded every expectation we had.

The quality of the food was truly outstanding. Every course was presented like a work of art, and the flavors were exceptionally clean, fresh, and balanced. The service was equally impressive, the team took the time to explain each dish, including where every ingredient was sourced. We were blown away by the beautiful, meaty, and sweet Hokkaido scallops, as well as the gorgeous fish flown in from Japan and Nova Scotia.

Sitting at the Chef’s table and watching Chef Masayoshi craft each piece with such precision and care made the evening even more special. It felt intimate, thoughtful, and elevated without being pretentious.

Everything from the pacing to the presentation to the hospitality was excellent. This was truly a night we will always remember!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "If food place could resemble a spa, this is it. Master Masa's gentle character is felt everywhere, and it was heavenly. I was cozy while feeling slightly nervous at the beginning (probably because I had no clue what to expect).

Portions are pretty small. So if you are one of those that require quantity over quality, this is not for you.

Experience the food artistry and taste each ingredients.

Bar seats recommended 100% for full experience. The subtle scents you find as dishes are being prepared was a bonus."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Came here for my dad's birthday.  My dad loves sushi, but my mom, son, and I aren't really fond of eating raw fish.  Soup was a bit bland, but the taste started to pick up once we hit the snow crab dish.  My son doesn't like to eat crab, but even he enjoyed it.  I love crab and wouldn't mind if my entire meal was that dish.  The nigiri was really well done.  I was expecting/hoping for more seared varieties, but there were a lot of raw ones, which I also enjoyed.  Service was also excellent.  The dinner was a bit pricy, but the experience was unforgettable."
